data.title: The Privacy RWA protocol PRIVA will launch its IDO tonight at 8:00 PM.
data.description: According to official news, PRIVA, a privacy-driven autonomous platform for Real-World Assets (RWA), will officially launch its Initiated Domination (IDD) at 8:00 PM tonight (January 10th). PRIVA aims to address the current pain point in the RWA (Real-World Asset) sector where asset ownership verification and privacy protection are difficult to achieve simultaneously. By introducing a tiered node reward model and privacy protection technologies, PRIVA attempts to build a compliant and decentralized on-chain asset ecosystem.

data.title: Planet Evening News | January 10
data.description: 1. Galaxy Research Director interprets the prospects of the crypto infrastructure bill vote: Passage would be a major positive, while obstruction or suppression of sentiment; 2. Vitalik: "Corposlop" is a soulless, evil, and clumsy homogenization; 3. A whale that had been dormant for a year withdrew 80,000 SOL from Binance 5 hours ago, worth $10.87 million; 4. Paul Chan: Cryptocurrency is part of financial innovation, and Hong Kong should embrace it but must handle it cautiously; 5. CZ: Complaining about others not being decentralized enough is a manifestation of one's own centralized thinking; 6. Huatai Securities: Expects the Federal Reserve to pause interest rate cuts from January to May, and then cut rates 1-2 times after the new chairman takes office; 7. data.title: Pi Network Pushes Real-World Utility With 10-Minute Payment Integration Tool
data.description:
The new library supports widely used frontend and backend frameworks, including JavaScript, React, Next. js, and Ruby on Rails. This broad compatibility allows developers to plug Pi payments into existing stacks without reworking application architecture. Integration connects directly to the Pi Mainnet, which moved to an Open Network on February 20, 2025

data.title: UN Taps Tether to Battle Crypto Scams and Human Trafficking
data.description:
Tether announced a partnership with the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime to fund cybersecurity bootcamps in Senegal, trafficking victim assistance across six African nations, and blockchain education in Papua New Guinea, shifting from an enforcement model that froze $3. 3 billion across 7,268 addresses to development-focused initiatives.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/un-taps-tether-to-battle-crypto-scams-and-human-trafficking/

data.title: The SONIC main team wallet transferred 12 million tokens, worth approximately $1 million, to a new address.
data.description: According to OnchainSchool monitoring, about four hours ago, the SONIC main team's wallet transferred 12 million tokens, worth approximately $1 million, to a new address. Another 12 million SONIC tokens were transferred to the same address five hours ago, and that address now holds approximately $2 million worth of SONIC tokens. In addition, on-chain data suggests that the SONIC main team may have begun withdrawing tokens from Wintermute and several exchanges over the past week.

data.title: Institutions: Markets are pricing in a Fed rate cut in January, with the earliest possible rate cut in June.
data.description: A research report from Founder Securities stated that the December non-farm payroll data was mixed, with the US job market generally showing a mild downward trend, but the unemployment rate showed marginal improvement, giving the Federal Reserve more reason to wait and see in January. Combined with the Supreme Court's potential declaration that the IEEPA tariffs are unconstitutional, this may be a short-term positive for US stocks and the US dollar, but a negative for US Treasuries. Data on new jobs, job vacancy rates, and hourly wage growth indicate that the US job market remained relatively weak in December, but the marginal decline in the unemployment rate was one of the few bright spots. Looking at the trends in interest rate futures and US Treasuries, the market priced in a Fed rate cut in January, with a possible rate cut as early as June. Meanwhile, the Supreme Court's potential declaration that the IEEPA tariffs are unconstitutional means that economic expectations may improve marginally, inflationary pressures may weaken, but the fiscal deficit may worsen. With the Fed in no hurry to cut rates and tariffs easing, US Treasuries face many unfavorable factors in the short term and are likely to remain at high levels. US stocks will benefit from the AI ​​boom and reduced tariff disruptions, especially in sectors affected by tariffs such as consumer staples and industry, which are more resilient. (Jinshi)

data.title: Best compliant crypto casinos for US and EU players
data.description:
The United States of America has not licensed any online crypto casino; most regulations are at the state level. Similar terms apply in Europe, where each member state has its own policies for online casinos. This situation leaves many US and EU players to seek offshore digital gambling platforms licensed by international jurisdictions like Curaçao. […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/compliant-crypto-casinos-for-us-and-eu/

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Surpasses 3,100 USDT with a 0.09% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jan 10, 2026, 10:49 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has crossed the 3,100 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 3,100 USDT, with a narrowed 0.09% increase in 24 hours.
